3d_model.notes: 3D massing model shows single-story civic building with central vestibule atrium, library wings arranged around a courtyard, and prominent accessible entry facing the public street. Solar panels visible on roof plane. Civic scale and character expressed through generous lobby volume and civic art niche at entrance.

renders.notes: 4 exterior perspectives: main entry facade, courtyard view, street-level approach, and aerial massing. Civic materials (concrete, local stone accent, shade structure) shown. Accessibility ramp and parking clearly visible.

material_direction.notes: Direction board presents: exposed architectural concrete (primary structure), regional stone accent (entry and civic art wall), high-durability ceramic tile (public floors), wood-tone acoustic panels (reading rooms), solar panel integration (roof). Emphasis on durability and maintainability for public use.

color_direction.notes: Neutral base palette (concrete grey, warm stone, natural wood tone) with civic accent color (municipal palette compliant). Color differentiates adult and children's zones. Public circulation in neutral for clarity and maintenance.

space_arrangement.notes: Floor plan shows all 10 programmed spaces from area-program.json: sala lectura adultos (400m²), sala lectura infantil (200m²), acervo cerrado (300m²), sala cómputo (150m²), sala usos múltiples (200m²), administración (150m²), sanitarios (4×30m² = 120m²), vestíbulo (200m²), estacionamiento (250m²), bodega (30m²). Total 2,000m² confirmed. All public spaces fully accessible from arrival.

review_notes: Concept fully satisfies the approved 2,000m² area program with all 10 programmed spaces represented at correct sizes. Civic character is strong — the accessible entry, courtyard, and civic art wall respond directly to the public mandate. Solar integration is clearly shown on roof and will carry through to engineering coordination. All spaces meet accessibility compliance requirements from the concept stage. Recommend approval.
